raw_consumer_statement:
I was wrongly charged the same amount five different times on the same Citi credit card ( in a matter of seconds ). The merchant in the end charged the same amount using a different system on my XXXX credit card. In total, I had the same amount billed six times over across two different credit cards ( five on Citi and one with XXXX ). 


I contacted Citi to reverse all five transactions, filled out all the forms they wanted me to and also sent them a screenshot of the XXXX transaction proving I did pay the merchant. Citi did not look like they bothered going through all the documentation I had submitted. I'm still getting the run around from them. 


In a hilarious twist, I even have a letter from Citi saying " The letter you recently sent to us was damaged or separated during processing. Please send us another signed letter restating your inquiry. '' The letter I sent was a pdf attachment in an email! That got fixed when I resent the same email with the same attachment. 


The latest development is that I have resent a pdf of the list of transactions from XXXX 's website.
